#  Deactivate a Bank Account as a Payee 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
##### Limited Release
[Free Procore accounts](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/free-procore-account/tutorials/create-a-free-procore-company-account) are currently available in English in Canada and the United States.
##  Background 
If you are contracted to do a job for a company using Procore Pay to manage invoice payments, you can [add a bank account](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payee-setup-guide/add-your-bank-account) to your company's account to receive payments. You can add multiple accounts and deactivate accounts as needed, however, you must always have one (1) bank account linked as a deposit account.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)  
 _Note:_ Permissions differ based on whether your company has a free or paid Procore account. See [How can I tell if I have a free or paid Procore account?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-can-i-tell-if-i-have-a-free-or-paid-procore-account)
  * You must link one (1) bank account and set it as your 'Deposit Account' to receive payments with Procore Pay.


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to your company's homepage. See [How do I change companies?](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/login-and-account-management/tutorials/change-companies-in-procores-navigation-bar)
  2. Navigate to the **Payments** tool.
  3. Click the **Configure Settings** ![Icons Settings Gear](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/8d4e4403c0eed73423d93c414c08e3a6/35b17d2e5497c876221891c1d9e5a114/icons-settings-gear.png?fm=webp&h=15) icon.
  4. Click **External Bank Accounts**.
  5. Click the **Vertical Ellipsis** ![Icon Ellipsis Vertical](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b6d0fa56ca6a66941a4032499f56aec8/2bb6315cb1db00c907bda1e2ae7a081c/icon-vertical-ellipsis.png?fm=webp&h=15) next to the bank account and click **Deactivate**.
  6. Review the confirmation prompt. 
#####  Important
Before you deactivate your external bank account, be aware of the following:
     * If the account being deactivated is also your deposit account, you must first set up a new deposit account. See [Edit a Bank Account as a Payee](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/edit-a-bank-account-as-a-payee).
     * By deactivating the external account, it will no longer be available to receive payments. Any payments that have already been sent to this account will not be canceled.
     * Once deactivated, no new payments can be deposited in the account.
  7. If you agree, click **Deactivate**.


## See Also
  * [Edit a Bank Account as a Payee](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/edit-a-bank-account-as-a-payee)
  * [Procore Pay - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
  * [Procore Pay - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
title: Deactivate a Bank Account as a Payee
description: How to deactivate a bank account that your company added to receive payments processed by Procore Pay.
